rationale: The page provides a comprehensive visual and conceptual explanation of supply and demand shifts and how inflation affects price elasticity across different categories of goods including necessities, luxuries, durables, and inferior goods.
cleanedText: 5. GROWTH AND DEVELOPMENT 5.1. THE ECONOMICS OF PRICE THE ECONOMICS OF PRICE: SUPPLY & DEMAND RELATIONSHIPS * Scenario A: Demand Increases - Demand curve shifts right (D1 to D2). Price increases, Quantity increases. * Scenario B: Demand Decreases - Demand curve shifts left (D1 to D2). Price decreases, Quantity decreases. * Scenario C: Supply Increases - Supply curve shifts right (S1 to S2). Price decreases, Quantity increases. * Scenario D: Supply Decreases - Supply curve shifts left (S1 to S2). Price increases, Quantity decreases. * Legend: Blue Lines & Arrows = Demand Shifts; Orange Lines & Arrows = Supply Shifts; Up Arrow = Increase; Down Arrow = Decrease. 5.2. INFLATION & PRICE ELASTICITY HOW INFLATION & GOOD TYPE AFFECT PRICE ELASTICITY: The 'Wallet Squeeze' & Its Impact on Consumer Choices. * Concept: Inflation (Reduced Real Income) leads to the 'Wallet Squeeze' where consumers become generally more price sensitive (Elasticity Increases) for non-essentials. * Impact by Good Type: 1. A. NECESSITY (Inelastic): Normal Behavior: Food, Fuel, Medicine (Buy regardless of price). Behavior During Inflation: Brand Switch (Premium to Cheaper Alternative) to save budget for other goods. 2. B. LUXURY / DISCRETIONARY (Elastic): Normal Behavior: Designer Clothes, Vacations (Sensitive to price). Behavior During Inflation: Hyper-Elastic; sales plummet as these are the first to be cut. 3. C. DURABLE GOODS (Postponable): Normal Behavior: Cars, Appliances (Purchase decision). Behavior During Inflation: Delayed; consumers make old goods last and postpone purchases. 4. D. INFERIOR GOODS (Trading Down): Normal Behavior: Generic Spam, Instant Noodles (Bought when income is low). Behavior During Inflation: Demand Increases; consumers switch to these from expensive options. * Legend: Steep Curve = Inelastic; Flat Curve = Elastic; Arrow = Shift in Demand/Behavior.
